steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 claims Annibale Carracci (c. 1585) was “the first artist to depict themself painting” in Bologna. The question asks how the author of Text 2 would respond to that underlined claim. The answer must reflect what Text 2 actually argues — that Fontana’s 1577 painting is the earliest Bolognese example, eight years earlier than Carracci’s.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Carracci and Fontana were among the most well-respected painters in Bologna at the time.: neither text mentions the reputation or social standing of either painter, so this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — The depiction of Fontana in Self-Portrait at the Keyboard was intended to underscore the artist's creativity.: Text 2 says nothing about Fontana’s artistic intent; this has no connection to the claim about who was first. ✗
  • Choice C — Fontana likely inspired the reference to an easel and palette in Carracci's painting.: Text 2 does not suggest any influence between the two painters; it is only concerned with chronological priority, not inspiration. ✗
  • Choice D — Self-Portrait at the Keyboard was painted earlier than Carracci's painting and also refers to the artist's craft.: Text 2 establishes both facts — Fontana’s 1577 date precedes Carracci’s c. 1585, and the easel in the background references the artistic craft — directly contradicting the “first” claim. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
